title: Hunter.io Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Hunter.io** is an email-discovery + verification + lead-management SaaS exposing a REST API under `https://api.hunter.io/v2`. This connector — `HunterConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "hunter"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically uses to research, verify, and pipeline B2B contacts:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Account | `/account` | Returns plan, requests remaining, team info |
| Domain Search | `/domain-search` | List all email addresses Hunter knows for a domain / company |
| Email Finder | `/email-finder` | Guess the most likely email for a person at a domain |
| Email Verifier | `/email-verifier` | SMTP-level deliverability + risk verdict |
| Email Count | `/email-count` | Count of public emails Hunter knows for a domain |
| Leads | `/leads` | List + read + create + update + delete leads |
| Lead Lists | `/leads_lists` | List + create lead-lists (folders for leads) |
| Campaigns | `/campaigns` | List outbound email campaigns |

The connector normalises **leads** into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces one standalone public `async def` method per Hunter operation (OCP), retries 429 + 5xx with exponential backoff that honours `Retry-After`, and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::HunterHTTPClient`).

**Hunter-specific gotcha:** the API key is passed as a `?api_key=<key>` **query parameter** — NOT a header. The HTTP client owns this detail; consumers of `HunterConnector` never see the key on the wire.

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate a Hunter.io API key

1. Sign in to https://hunter.io.
2. Open **Dashboard → API → API Key**.
3. Copy the key. Hunter shows the key in full each visit, but rotate it via the same screen if it ever leaks.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Hunter.io**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `api_key` (secret) | API key from step 3 |
| `base_url` (string, optional) | Override `https://api.hunter.io/v2` (rarely needed) |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft cap, default 60 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `api_key` is present (it does NOT call the API), initialises the `HunterHTTPClient`, stores a `TokenInfo` whose `access_token` is the api_key, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, CONNECTED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which probes `GET /account`. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 → `DEGRADED + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On 403 → `UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 429 → `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with structured-log warning. On network failure → `OFFLINE + CONNECTED`.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Hunter REST API uses **API-key authentication via a query parameter** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). No OAuth dance, no token refresh, no expiry.

### Credential model

- `api_key` is sent as `?api_key=<key>` on every request — **NOT** as an `Authorization` header.
- The HTTP client's `_params()` helper appends `api_key` **last** so a caller cannot accidentally clobber the auth token via the `params` kwarg.
- `None` values are dropped from query params so `httpx` never emits `?domain=None`.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `api_key`. Calls `save_config()` + `set_token()`. Does **not** call any Hunter endpoint. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a `TokenInfo(access_token=api_key, token_type="ApiKey")` for ABI compatibility — no real exchange. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /account?api_key=<key>` — minimal probe that also verifies the key. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — no token to refresh. |
| token storage | `set_token()` records the api_key once at install — purely for framework consistency. |

### Wire contract

```
GET  https://api.hunter.io/v2/<path>?api_key=<key>&<other-params>
POST https://api.hunter.io/v2/<path>?api_key=<key>      body: application/json
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `api_key` | secret | yes | `self.api_key` | Query-param token on every request |
| `base_url` | string | no | `self.base_url` (defaults to `https://api.hunter.io/v2`) | Override for sandbox / proxy |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 60) | Soft client-side ceiling — Hunter also enforces its own quota |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
CONNECTOR_TYPE = "hunter"
CONNECTOR_NAME = "Hunter.io"
AUTH_TYPE      = "api_key"
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["api_key"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("DEGRADED",  "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_HUNTER_BASE = "https://api.hunter.io/v2"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `HunterConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::HunterHTTPClient`. Each method auto-injects the configured `api_key` into the query string.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `api_key`; persist config; set token. Does NOT call the API.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key auth — returns a TokenInfo whose access_token is the configured key.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Probes `GET /account` and classifies failures via `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Paginates `GET /leads` (100-per-page) → `normalize_lead()` → `ingest_document`. When `since` is provided, leads with older `created_at` are skipped client-side.

children.title: Account
children.content:
### `async get_account() -> Dict`
`GET /account` — returns plan, requests remaining, team membership.

children.title: Email Discovery
children.content:
### `async domain_search(domain=None, company=None, limit=25, offset=0, type=None, seniority=None, department=None) -> Dict`
`GET /domain-search?domain=…&limit=…&offset=…&type=…&seniority=…&department=…`.

### `async email_finder(domain=None, company=None, first_name=None, last_name=None, full_name=None, max_duration=10) -> Dict`
`GET /email-finder?domain=…&first_name=…&last_name=…`.

### `async email_verifier(email) -> Dict`
`GET /email-verifier?email=…`.

### `async email_count(domain=None, company=None, type=None) -> Dict`
`GET /email-count?domain=…`.

children.title: Leads CRUD
children.content:
### `async list_leads(offset=0, limit=20, lead_list_id=None, email=None, domain=None, company=None) -> Dict`
`GET /leads?offset=…&limit=…&lead_list_id=…`.

### `async get_lead(lead_id) -> Dict`
`GET /leads/{lead_id}`.

### `async create_lead(email=None, first_name=None, last_name=None, company=None, lead_list_id=None, source=None) -> Dict`
`POST /leads` — body: only fields the caller supplied (None values omitted). NOTE: Hunter's payload key for `lead_list_id` on writes is `leads_list_id` (with the trailing s); the connector handles this translation.

### `async update_lead(lead_id, fields) -> Dict`
`PUT /leads/{lead_id}` — body is the `fields` dict verbatim.

### `async delete_lead(lead_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /leads/{lead_id}`.

children.title: Lead Lists + Campaigns
children.content:
### `async list_lead_lists(offset=0, limit=20) -> Dict`
`GET /leads_lists`.

### `async create_lead_list(name, team_id=None) -> Dict`
`POST /leads_lists` — body: `{name, team_id?}`.

### `async list_campaigns(offset=0, limit=20) -> Dict`
`GET /campaigns`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
HunterError                           # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── HunterAuthError                   # 401 / 403
├── HunterNotFoundError               # 404
├── HunterRateLimitError              # 429 — retry_after_s
├── HunterServerError                 # 5xx
└── HunterNetworkError                # httpx transport (DNS, connect, timeout)
```

Back-compat alias preserved:
```
HunterNotFound = HunterNotFoundError  # legacy name from older code
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 | Raise `HunterError` immediately |
| 401 / 403 | Raise `HunterAuthError` immediately |
| 404 | Raise `HunterNotFoundError` immediately |
| 429 | Exponential backoff `(2^attempt) + jitter` (capped at 30s), honour `Retry-After`, retry up to `_max_retries=3`; raise `HunterRateLimitError` if exhausted |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff, retry up to 3; raise `HunterServerError` if exhausted |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.ConnectError` / `ReadError` / `WriteError` | Backoff + retry up to 3, then raise `HunterNetworkError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
HunterAuthError      → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  TOKEN_EXPIRED)
HunterNetworkError   → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,   CONNECTED)
HunterRateLimitError → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)
HunterError          → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `api_key` blank | Re-enter the key in ACP. The Hunter Dashboard → API screen always shows it; rotate if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 from Hunter — key invalid or rotated | Verify the key in Hunter Dashboard → API. Paste it again in ACP and retry. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 from Hunter — usually means the plan quota is exhausted or the team disabled the key | Check **Dashboard → API → Requests** — the monthly meter resets on plan renewal. Upgrade or wait. |
| `hunter.http.retryable_status` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (Hunter per-minute rate cap) | The client retries with exponential backoff that honours `Retry-After`. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` in ACP, or batch fewer concurrent requests. |
| 404 on `get_lead(lead_id=N)` | The lead was deleted or belongs to a different team | Confirm the lead ID by listing leads first; check team membership. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'HunterNotFound'` | Older code expecting the legacy alias | Already covered — `HunterNotFound = HunterNotFoundError` alias is exported from `exceptions.py`. |
| `api_key` accidentally appears in shared structured logs | Caller passed `api_key=...` through `params=` themselves | The HTTP client's `_params()` helper never logs the merged dict; check the caller. Always pass auth-sensitive values via the dedicated `api_key` argument. |
| `sync()` reports 0 documents on a populated account | The `/leads` endpoint is filtered by team membership tied to the key | Re-issue the API key under a team that has lead-list access. |
